full_prompt: Develop a primal-dual algorithm for large-scale conic optimization with time-varying constraints, where feasibility may be temporarily violated. Analyze dynamic regret, constraint violation, and convergence under Slater-type conditions.

Study nonconvex stochastic optimization problems satisfying a Polyak-Lojasiewicz condition only locally. Design adaptive stepsize schemes that provably enter the favorable region with high probability.

Investigate distributionally robust optimization over Wasserstein balls for mixed-integer linear programs with endogenous uncertainty. Derive tractable reformulations or valid cutting planes with finite-sample guarantees.
Develop decomposition methods for two-stage stochastic mixed-integer programs with nonconvex recourse. Compare logic-based Benders cuts, integer L-shaped cuts, and convexification-based approaches.

Analyze accelerated first-order methods for composite convex optimization when proximal operators are computed inexactly by iterative subroutines. Establish error tolerances that preserve optimal convergence rates.

Design global optimization algorithms for quadratically constrained quadratic programs using semidefinite relaxations strengthened by problem-specific valid inequalities. Characterize when the relaxation is exact.

Study the complexity of finding second-order stationary points in constrained nonconvex optimization with nonlinear equality and inequality constraints. Develop algorithms with explicit dependence on constraint qualification constants.
Formulate robust network flow problems with correlated arc-capacity uncertainty and budgeted ambiguity sets. Derive compact or extended formulations and analyze the price of robustness.

Investigate bilevel optimization models for pricing and capacity allocation in transportation or energy systems. Develop single-level reformulations using complementarity conditions and study tractable convex relaxations.

Develop variance-reduced stochastic gradient methods for nonconvex finite-sum problems with nonsmooth regularizers. Prove convergence to stationary points under weak assumptions on minibatch sampling.
Study cutting-plane algorithms for convex optimization with expensive separation oracles. Analyze tradeoffs between oracle accuracy, localization volume reduction, and iteration complexity.
Model multi-period inventory control with service-level constraints as a convex or mixed-integer convex optimization problem under demand ambiguity. Derive structural properties of optimal policies.
Investigate low-rank matrix factorization as a nonconvex approach to semidefinite programming. Identify conditions under which all second-order stationary points correspond to global optima.

Develop scalable algorithms for chance-constrained optimization with dependent, heavy-tailed uncertainty. Compare scenario approximation, sample-average approximation, and distributionally robust surrogates.
Study online convex optimization with long-term constraints and delayed feedback. Design algorithms with simultaneous sublinear regret and sublinear cumulative constraint violation.

Analyze nonconvex augmented Lagrangian methods for problems with rank constraints, sparsity constraints, or complementarity constraints. Establish convergence guarantees under realistic constraint qualifications.

Develop branch-and-cut algorithms for vehicle routing problems with stochastic travel times and time-window reliability constraints. Propose valid inequalities exploiting route structure and uncertainty sets.

Investigate convex relaxations for AC optimal power flow using second-order cone and semidefinite programming hierarchies. Characterize network topologies and loading regimes where relaxations are tight.
Study multiobjective convex optimization with preference uncertainty. Develop scalarization or set-valued methods that provide approximation guarantees for the Pareto frontier.
Design proximal bundle methods for nonsmooth convex optimization when subgradients are noisy or biased. Analyze stability, convergence rates, and practical stopping criteria.
Develop algorithms for sparse nonconvex empirical risk minimization with cardinality constraints. Compare mixed-integer formulations, continuous relaxations, and iterative hard-thresholding methods.

Study queueing-network control as a stochastic optimization problem with convex holding costs and nonconvex capacity decisions. Develop policies with provable heavy-traffic optimality or performance bounds.
Investigate Lagrangian relaxation and column generation for large-scale workforce scheduling with fairness, skill-mix, and uncertainty constraints. Analyze dual bounds and stabilization strategies.

Develop sample-efficient methods for black-box nonconvex optimization with expensive simulation-based objectives. Combine Bayesian optimization with trust-region or derivative-free optimization techniques and derive convergence guarantees.

Study parametric convex optimization problems arising in real-time decision systems. Develop sensitivity analysis and warm-start methods that exploit problem structure to achieve fast repeated solves.

Investigate finite-blocklength achievability and converse bounds for channels with memory under mismatched decoding. Characterize the second-order coding rate and dispersion for representative Markov and hidden-Markov channel models.

Develop polar coding schemes for non-stationary channels whose transition laws vary slowly over time. Analyze polarization speed, rate loss due to adaptation, and robustness to imperfect channel-state estimation.

Study the capacity region of multi-terminal semantic communication systems under task-oriented distortion measures. Compare classical rate-distortion optimality with performance criteria based on inference accuracy or decision risk.

Derive tight non-asymptotic bounds for distributed source coding with side information when sources exhibit long-range dependence. Examine whether standard Slepian-Wolf random binning remains optimal under practical decoding constraints.

Design list-decodable codes for adversarial channels with both symbol errors and synchronization errors. Establish tradeoffs among list size, redundancy, insertion-deletion fraction, and computational decoding complexity.

Analyze the role of information density concentration in neural-network-based channel coding. Determine whether learned codes approach known finite-blocklength bounds on AWGN, fading, and non-linear channels.

Develop coding strategies for molecular communication channels with inter-symbol interference and molecule degradation. Quantify capacity scaling under realistic diffusion models, receptor noise, and biochemical constraints.

Investigate universal lossless compression for data generated by large language models or other high-dimensional generative processes. Characterize redundancy rates when the source distribution is implicit and only samples are available.

Construct error-correcting codes optimized for DNA storage channels with substitution, insertion, deletion, and synthesis bias. Analyze capacity and practical code design under sequencing coverage constraints.

Study information-theoretic limits of private federated learning under communication constraints. Derive lower bounds relating mutual information leakage, model accuracy, number of clients, and uplink coding rates.

Explore capacity-achieving codes for compound wiretap channels with uncertainty in both legitimate and eavesdropper channel laws. Develop secrecy metrics that remain meaningful in finite-blocklength regimes.

Analyze feedback capacity for channels with delayed, noisy, or quantized feedback. Determine when variable-length coding with stop feedback yields substantial reliability gains over fixed-length schemes.

Develop joint source-channel coding methods for real-time control over unreliable networks. Characterize stability, delay, and distortion tradeoffs for linear dynamical systems transmitted over packet-erasure or fading channels.

Investigate coded caching schemes under heterogeneous user demands and nonuniform file popularities. Derive converses that account for finite file sizes, decentralized placement, and privacy constraints.

Study the information bottleneck method in the high-dimensional asymptotic regime. Establish rigorous links between bottleneck tradeoffs, generalization error, and rate-distortion theory for supervised learning.

Design locally repairable codes with simultaneous security against eavesdropping and resilience to node failures. Determine optimal distance, locality, repair bandwidth, and secrecy-rate tradeoffs for distributed storage.
Analyze quantum error-correcting codes for biased noise and correlated error models. Develop decoding algorithms that exploit noise structure and compare thresholds against surface-code baselines.

Investigate zero-error capacity for graph-based channel models with feedback and shared randomness. Characterize when feedback increases Shannon capacity and relate results to graph entropy and Lovász theta functions.

Develop coding theorems for age-of-information constrained communication systems. Determine optimal sampling, compression, and transmission policies when freshness rather than average distortion is the primary performance metric.

Study approximate message passing and spatial coupling for sparse superposition codes over general channels. Prove threshold saturation results and quantify finite-length scaling under practical decoding schedules.

Derive rate-distortion functions for graph-structured and manifold-valued data under topology-aware distortion measures. Apply the theory to compression of networks, point clouds, and sensor-field observations.

Analyze covert communication over multiple-access and relay channels under statistical detection constraints. Characterize square-root-law scaling, achievable coding schemes, and the impact of shared secret keys.

Investigate information-theoretic limits of split inference, where intermediate neural representations are transmitted over noisy links. Optimize representation dimension, channel code rate, and task loss jointly.

Develop algebraic and probabilistic constructions of rank-metric codes for network coding with adversarial packet injections. Establish efficient decoding guarantees for large finite fields and subspace channel models.

Study universal channel coding with unknown input constraints such as cost, peak power, or sparsity. Determine minimax redundancy and construct practical codes that adapt without sacrificing reliability exponents.

Develop a finite-sample inference procedure for high-dimensional generalized linear models under misspecified link functions. Compare debiased estimating equations, sample splitting, and bootstrap calibration when covariates exhibit strong dependence.

Study the robustness of conformal prediction intervals under covariate shift with estimated density ratios. Derive coverage guarantees when the importance weights are learned from high-dimensional nuisance models.

Construct semiparametric confidence intervals for average treatment effects with clustered interference. Characterize how network sparsity, cluster size imbalance, and exposure mapping misspecification affect asymptotic validity.
Design an adaptive randomized experiment for heterogeneous treatment effect estimation under budget constraints. Analyze regret, inferential bias, and post-adaptive confidence interval coverage.

Investigate double machine learning estimators for causal mediation analysis with continuous mediators. Derive conditions under which orthogonal scores remain valid when mediator and outcome nuisance functions are estimated flexibly.
Develop randomization-based inference methods for factorial experiments with noncompliance. Compare sharp-null and weak-null testing procedures under monotonicity and exclusion restrictions.

Propose a Bayesian hierarchical model for multi-site randomized trials with site-level treatment effect heterogeneity. Examine shrinkage-induced bias and frequentist coverage of posterior credible intervals.
Analyze the impact of outcome-adaptive randomization on covariate balance and causal effect inference. Derive correction methods for valid inference after response-driven allocation changes.

Develop sensitivity analysis methods for unmeasured confounding in longitudinal marginal structural models. Quantify how time-varying confounder feedback alters bias bounds for inverse probability weighted estimators.

Compare targeted maximum likelihood estimation and augmented inverse probability weighting for treatment effects with rare outcomes. Study second-order remainder terms and finite-sample behavior under near-positivity violations.

Formulate optimal experimental designs for estimating interaction effects in high-dimensional factorial experiments. Incorporate sparsity assumptions and derive minimax lower bounds for detection power.
Develop permutation tests for causal effects in matched observational studies with continuous doses. Characterize validity under imperfect matching and derive sensitivity analyses for hidden bias.

Study inference for regression discontinuity designs with multiple cutoffs and heterogeneous treatment effects. Develop bias-corrected estimators that account for cutoff-specific density discontinuities and bandwidth selection uncertainty.

Investigate causal effect estimation in panel data with staggered adoption and treatment effect heterogeneity. Compare synthetic control, difference-in-differences, and imputation estimators under violations of parallel trends.

Develop methods for estimating spillover effects in two-stage randomized experiments on networks. Derive variance estimators that remain valid under partial interference and degree-dependent treatment assignment.

Construct confidence sets for causal estimands identified by instrumental variables with weak and invalid instruments. Combine overidentification restrictions, sensitivity parameters, and robust Anderson-Rubin-type tests.

Study selective inference after model selection in randomized experiments with many baseline covariates. Derive valid confidence intervals for treatment effects when covariate adjustment is chosen using data-adaptive procedures.

Develop optimal stopping rules for sequential A/B tests with multiple metrics and delayed outcomes. Control familywise error or false discovery rates under continuous monitoring and dependence among endpoints.

Analyze missing outcome data in randomized trials using principal stratification and sensitivity models. Compare likelihood-based, weighting-based, and Bayesian approaches under nonignorable attrition.

Design estimators for causal effects under interference in marketplace experiments with equilibrium feedback. Model how treatment assignment changes prices, matching rates, or congestion, and derive estimands with policy relevance.

Develop distribution-free inference for quantile treatment effects using rank-based randomization tests. Extend the framework to stratified and clustered randomized designs with unequal assignment probabilities.

Investigate the efficiency of covariate-adjusted estimators in cluster-randomized trials with small numbers of clusters. Derive bias corrections and degrees-of-freedom adjustments for generalized estimating equations.

Propose a framework for transportability of randomized trial results to target populations using observational covariates. Derive doubly robust estimators and assess sensitivity to selection mechanisms and lack of overlap.

Study causal discovery methods for time series with latent confounders and feedback loops. Develop statistical tests for directed dependence that control false positives under autocorrelation and nonstationarity.

Develop experimental design criteria for estimating dynamic treatment regimes in micro-randomized trials. Optimize randomization probabilities over time to balance estimation precision, participant burden, and ethical constraints.

Develop a nonlinear model predictive control scheme for input-constrained systems with state-dependent uncertainty and prove recursive feasibility under tube-based tightening. Compare performance against robust MPC with polytopic uncertainty sets on a benchmark underactuated system.

Investigate contraction-based control for high-dimensional nonlinear systems with learned dynamics models. Derive sufficient conditions under which approximation error in a neural network dynamics model preserves exponential incremental stability.

Design distributed controllers for multi-agent systems with switching communication graphs, communication delays, and heterogeneous agent dynamics. Establish consensus or formation-tracking guarantees using common or multiple Lyapunov functions.

Study safety-critical control using control barrier functions for systems with relative-degree greater than one and actuator saturation. Formulate conditions ensuring forward invariance of safe sets when barrier constraints are implemented through quadratic programs.

Analyze the stability of hybrid dynamical systems with state-triggered switching and reset maps. Derive dwell-time or average dwell-time conditions that prevent Zeno behavior while guaranteeing asymptotic stability.

Develop data-driven system identification methods that preserve passivity and dissipativity properties of the true plant. Use the identified model to synthesize controllers with certified closed-loop stability margins.

Investigate Koopman operator-based control for nonlinear systems with control-affine dynamics. Construct finite-dimensional lifted representations and quantify the impact of basis truncation on closed-loop stability.

Formulate robust adaptive control laws for nonlinear systems with unmatched uncertainties and time-varying parameters. Prove boundedness and convergence properties using composite adaptation and dynamic regressor extension techniques.

Study optimal control of stochastic differential equations with multiplicative noise and state constraints. Derive Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman conditions and compare numerical solutions using stochastic MPC and policy iteration.

Develop event-triggered control strategies for networked control systems subject to packet drops and variable sampling intervals. Prove input-to-state stability while minimizing communication frequency.

Analyze controllability and observability of temporal networks whose graph topology evolves according to a Markov process. Characterize how switching rates and graph connectivity affect the minimum control energy.

Design resilient control architectures for cyber-physical systems under false-data injection attacks and denial-of-service events. Establish detection and mitigation guarantees using unknown-input observers and robust feedback synthesis.

Investigate bifurcation control in nonlinear oscillatory systems with parametric uncertainty. Design feedback laws that shift or suppress Hopf, saddle-node, or period-doubling bifurcations while preserving desired equilibria.

Develop scalable algorithms for solving large-scale linear quadratic regulator problems with sparsity constraints on the feedback gain. Analyze suboptimality bounds and closed-loop stability for distributed implementations.

Study synchronization of coupled nonlinear oscillators with uncertain coupling strengths and time delays. Derive conditions for phase locking or complete synchronization using Lyapunov-Krasovskii functionals.

Formulate reachability analysis methods for nonlinear systems using zonotopes, polynomial approximations, or Hamilton-Jacobi techniques. Evaluate the tradeoff between computational complexity and conservatism in safety verification.

Develop adaptive dynamic programming methods for continuous-time nonlinear control systems with unknown drift dynamics. Prove convergence of value-function approximators and stability of the resulting policy updates.

Analyze the effect of quantization and finite word length on feedback stabilization of linear and nonlinear systems. Derive robustness margins that relate quantizer resolution to invariant set size and convergence rate.
Design observers for nonlinear systems with intermittent measurements and unknown inputs. Establish convergence guarantees using high-gain, sliding-mode, or moving-horizon estimation techniques.

Investigate port-Hamiltonian modeling and control of electromechanical or robotic systems with energy dissipation and constraints. Design passivity-based controllers that achieve stabilization while respecting physical interconnection structure.

Study mean-field control and mean-field games for large populations of interacting agents with nonlinear dynamics. Derive limiting equations and analyze stability of decentralized feedback policies as the number of agents grows.

Develop control methods for systems governed by partial differential equations, such as reaction-diffusion, fluid, or beam equations. Prove stabilization using boundary control, backstepping, or spectral methods.
Analyze inverse optimal control for nonlinear dynamical systems from trajectory data. Determine identifiability conditions for recovering cost functions and constraints from noisy demonstrations.

Design learning-enabled controllers with formal guarantees for uncertain nonlinear systems. Combine Gaussian process regression or neural networks with Lyapunov or barrier certificates to bound model error during online adaptation.

Investigate global stability and domain-of-attraction estimation for polynomial dynamical systems using sum-of-squares programming. Develop less conservative relaxations that scale to systems with many states and parameters.

Develop an a posteriori error estimator for adaptive finite element discretizations of nonlinear convection-diffusion-reaction equations in the advection-dominated regime. Analyze reliability, efficiency, and robustness with respect to the Péclet number.

Study well-posedness and numerical approximation of a coupled Navier-Stokes-Cahn-Hilliard system with degenerate mobility and variable density. Design energy-stable time-stepping schemes and prove convergence to weak solutions.

Formulate a PDE-constrained optimization problem for optimal actuator placement in parabolic systems with sparse controls. Investigate first-order optimality conditions and scalable algorithms based on semismooth Newton methods.

Analyze convergence rates of physics-informed neural networks for elliptic boundary value problems with low-regularity coefficients. Compare residual minimization in strong and weak forms under mesh-dependent sampling strategies.

Develop high-order discontinuous Galerkin schemes for hyperbolic conservation laws with nonconvex fluxes. Establish entropy stability and evaluate performance near shocks, rarefactions, and compound waves.

Investigate optimal transport-based preconditioning for large-scale convex optimization problems arising in inverse imaging. Derive complexity bounds and benchmark against primal-dual hybrid gradient methods.
Construct structure-preserving numerical methods for Hamiltonian PDEs with damping and stochastic forcing. Quantify long-time behavior, invariant measure approximation, and discrete energy balance.

Study multilevel Monte Carlo finite element methods for elliptic PDEs with lognormal random coefficients. Derive sample complexity estimates under limited spatial regularity and covariance truncation.

Develop reduced-order models for parametrized incompressible flow using operator inference with stability constraints. Analyze error propagation when training data are generated from under-resolved simulations.

Examine the Γ-convergence of phase-field approximations for topology optimization with perimeter regularization and PDE state constraints. Determine conditions under which discrete minimizers converge to sharp-interface designs.

Design fast solvers for saddle-point systems arising from mixed finite element discretizations of Darcy-Stokes coupling. Analyze block preconditioners robust with respect to viscosity contrast and mesh refinement.

Investigate second-order optimization methods for nonsmooth composite objectives involving total variation and box constraints. Develop globalization strategies and prove local superlinear convergence under partial smoothness assumptions.

Develop conservative semi-Lagrangian methods for kinetic Fokker-Planck equations in high dimensions. Study positivity preservation, entropy dissipation, and computational complexity under sparse-grid approximations.

Analyze homogenization limits for elliptic PDEs in perforated domains with nonlinear Robin boundary conditions. Derive effective interface laws and validate them numerically using multiscale finite element methods.

Formulate and solve an optimal control problem for epidemic reaction-diffusion models with spatially heterogeneous interventions. Characterize bang-bang and singular control regimes under budget constraints.

Develop randomized numerical linear algebra techniques for accelerating Newton-Krylov solvers in nonlinear PDE discretizations. Quantify how sketching affects residual accuracy, convergence, and preconditioner quality.
Study finite volume schemes for degenerate parabolic equations modeling porous medium flow with discontinuous permeability. Prove discrete maximum principles and convergence to entropy solutions.

Investigate bilevel optimization formulations for learning regularization parameters in inverse problems governed by elliptic PDEs. Derive differentiability of the solution map and compare implicit differentiation with adjoint-based gradients.

Construct exponential integrators for stiff reaction-diffusion systems with pattern-forming instabilities. Analyze stability regions, stiffness leakage, and accuracy near Turing bifurcation thresholds.
Develop domain decomposition methods for fractional Laplacian problems posed on bounded domains. Establish scalable preconditioners and compare integral, spectral, and extension-based formulations.
Analyze optimal control of stochastic parabolic PDEs driven by multiplicative noise. Derive stochastic maximum principles and design implementable algorithms using low-rank covariance approximations.

Study meshless radial basis function methods for nonlinear elliptic PDEs on manifolds. Establish consistency and stability estimates accounting for curvature, point cloud irregularity, and boundary effects.

Develop primal-dual algorithms for large-scale Wasserstein gradient flows discretized by finite elements. Prove convergence of the fully discrete scheme and investigate applications to aggregation-diffusion equations.

Investigate adaptive time-stepping strategies for coupled fluid-structure interaction problems with moving interfaces. Design error indicators that capture both geometric and PDE discretization errors.

Analyze the interplay between discretization error and optimization error in PDE-constrained inverse problems solved by inexact Gauss-Newton methods. Derive stopping criteria that balance mesh refinement, regularization, and iterative solver accuracy.

Investigate whether fine-grained lower bounds for dynamic reachability in directed graphs can be strengthened under variants of the Online Matrix-Vector multiplication conjecture. Focus on update/query tradeoffs when the graph has bounded treewidth or bounded feedback vertex set number.

Develop approximation algorithms for submodular maximization under matroid intersection constraints in the streaming model. Analyze whether adaptivity can be reduced without worsening the competitive ratio beyond current lower bounds.

Study the parameterized complexity of counting homomorphisms from bounded-depth pattern graphs into sparse host graphs. Determine whether new dichotomies emerge under nowhere-dense or bounded-expansion assumptions.

Explore derandomization of randomized incremental algorithms for geometric intersection problems. In particular, seek deterministic near-linear-time algorithms for low-dimensional arrangements with output-sensitive guarantees.

Analyze the communication complexity of distributed graph coloring in the CONGEST model under adversarial edge insertions and deletions. Establish lower bounds that separate dynamic distributed algorithms from static ones.

Investigate whether recent algebraic techniques for matrix multiplication yield improved algorithms for all-pairs shortest paths over small integer weights. Characterize barriers implied by min-plus product conjectures.

Develop new kernelization algorithms for deletion problems to hereditary graph classes defined by forbidden induced subgraphs. Identify structural conditions under which polynomial kernels are impossible under standard complexity assumptions.

Study the complexity of learning deterministic finite automata with membership and equivalence queries when counterexamples are noisy or adversarially truncated. Formalize robust convergence guarantees and lower bounds.

Design certified algorithms for numerical linear algebra whose outputs include machine-checkable proofs of spectral approximation. Connect randomized sketching methods with proof systems verifiable in near-linear time.

Investigate proof complexity lower bounds for cutting-plane systems augmented with semantic reasoning over small-width clauses. Determine whether such systems can efficiently refute random CSP instances near the satisfiability threshold.

Analyze the expressive power of fixed-point logics with counting on classes of sparse random graphs. Determine whether definability thresholds align with algorithmic tractability thresholds for associated decision problems.

Develop compositional verification methods for probabilistic programs with unbounded loops and continuous distributions. Seek sound and relatively complete proof rules for almost-sure termination and quantitative postconditions.

Study the complexity of model checking temporal logics with hyperproperties over asynchronous distributed systems. Identify fragments admitting polynomial-time verification under partial-order reduction.

Investigate synthesis of reactive controllers from specifications in first-order temporal logic over data streams. Determine decidable fragments and tight complexity bounds for bounded-memory implementations.

Explore the relationship between circuit lower bounds for small-depth threshold circuits and pseudorandom generators for streaming algorithms. Aim to translate new average-case lower bounds into explicit space-efficient PRGs.

Study quantum query complexity for graph property testing under promise restrictions such as planarity, bounded degree, or minor-freeness. Compare quantum speedups with classical lower bounds from communication complexity.

Develop algorithms for fair clustering with multiple protected attributes under metric uncertainty. Provide approximation guarantees robust to perturbations of the distance oracle and prove matching hardness results.

Investigate whether succinct data structures for dynamic strings can support pattern matching with errors in worst-case polylogarithmic update time. Establish conditional lower bounds from cell-probe complexity.

Analyze the descriptive complexity of constraint satisfaction problems over infinite-domain templates with polymorphisms. Seek new algebraic criteria for first-order definability, Datalog definability, and fixed-parameter tractability.

Develop automated invariant-generation techniques based on interpolation in theories combining arrays, arithmetic, and algebraic data types. Prove termination or convergence guarantees for well-structured classes of transition systems.

Study the sample complexity of property testing for regular languages when access is through random walks on automata-generated strings. Determine how syntactic monoid structure influences tester efficiency.

Investigate exact exponential-time algorithms for Hamiltonicity in directed graphs using inclusion-exclusion and rank-based dynamic programming. Characterize graph parameters under which base constants can be improved.
Develop semantic foundations for gradual verification in languages with dependent types and mutable state. Formalize consistency, blame, and preservation theorems for partially specified programs.

Study average-case hardness of lattice problems under distributions induced by cryptographic protocols rather than worst-case reductions. Determine whether standard reductions preserve fine-grained complexity parameters.

Investigate modular algorithms for SMT solving over nonlinear real arithmetic using combinations of symbolic abstraction and numerical certificates. Establish conditions under which returned unsatisfiability proofs are independently checkable.

Develop a dependent type theory with guarded recursion and quotient inductive-inductive types, and prove normalization or canonicity for a core fragment. Evaluate whether the system can encode coinductive bisimulation proofs without compromising decidable type checking.

Design a proof-producing model checker for CTL* over infinite-state transition systems using abstraction refinement. The prompt should include a formal account of how counterexamples are validated or refuted inside an interactive theorem prover.

Investigate the integration of SMT-based automation into a cubical type theory proof assistant. Formalize the soundness boundary between external solvers and internal proof terms, especially for equality, arithmetic, and finite datatypes.

Construct a certified verified compiler pass for a language with algebraic effects and handlers. Specify the operational semantics, prove semantic preservation, and mechanize the proof in Coq, Lean, or Isabelle/HOL.

Develop a temporal separation logic for reasoning about concurrent heap-manipulating programs with liveness properties. Prove soundness against an operational semantics with fair scheduling and demonstrate verification of a lock-free data structure.

Study decidable fragments of higher-order unification arising in elaboration for dependently typed languages. Characterize the fragment used by practical theorem provers and prove completeness or identify unavoidable incompleteness cases.

Create a framework for certified probabilistic model checking using measure-theoretic semantics in a theorem prover. Verify quantitative properties of Markov decision processes and prove correctness of numerical approximation algorithms.

Formalize a modal dependent type theory for distributed systems with explicit worlds, communication, and knowledge modalities. Prove metatheoretic properties such as substitution, preservation, and progress for a small distributed calculus.

Design a compositional verification method for parameterized protocols using inductive invariants and symmetry reduction. Prove soundness of the reduction and evaluate the method on cache coherence or consensus protocols.

Investigate proof irrelevance, uniqueness of identity proofs, and univalence in the presence of classical axioms. Identify which combinations are consistent in a given type-theoretic foundation and mechanize relative consistency results where possible.

Develop a certified extraction pipeline from constructive proofs to executable model checkers. Prove that extracted algorithms preserve the semantics of the temporal logics and transition systems formalized in the proof assistant.

Formalize and verify a saturation-based first-order theorem prover, including term indexing, simplification, and redundancy elimination. Prove refutational completeness for the implemented calculus and validate proof certificates independently.

Extend separation logic with dependent types for specifying memory layouts whose shape depends on runtime values. Prove a frame rule and soundness theorem, then apply the logic to verified serialization or parsing routines.

Construct an abstract interpretation framework inside a theorem prover that supports Galois connections, widening, and narrowing. Instantiate it for interval, polyhedral, or shape domains and prove soundness of the resulting analyzer.

Investigate the use of homotopy type theory for formalizing bisimulation and behavioral equivalence of transition systems. Compare higher-inductive quotient constructions with setoid-based encodings in terms of proof burden and computational content.

Develop a certified IC3/PDR model checking algorithm for finite-state safety properties. Formalize inductive frames, proof obligations, generalization steps, and prove that successful termination yields a valid safety proof.

Study refinement types with liquid inference for a functional language with recursion and algebraic datatypes. Prove soundness of constraint generation and investigate completeness for a decidable logical fragment.

Formalize a game-semantic model for a typed programming language with effects and prove full abstraction. Mechanize core parts of the adequacy and definability arguments in an interactive theorem prover.

Design a proof-carrying code architecture for WebAssembly or eBPF programs. Define a type-and-effect system or Hoare logic for safety properties and prove that independently checked certificates imply memory and control-flow safety.

Investigate certified equivalence checking for hardware circuits using bit-level SMT proofs and model checking. Develop a proof certificate format and verify a checker for combinational and sequential equivalence.

Develop a linear dependent type system for resource-aware verification of programs with memory, time, or energy bounds. Prove preservation of resource annotations under evaluation and demonstrate certified bounds for representative algorithms.

Formalize weak memory models such as ARMv8 or RISC-V in a theorem prover and verify compilation mappings from a concurrent source language. Prove that source-level data-race freedom or synchronization guarantees are preserved.

Create a verified runtime monitor synthesis procedure from temporal logic specifications. Prove that generated monitors are sound and complete for finite traces, and characterize limitations for liveness properties.

Investigate automated lemma discovery for inductive theorem proving using term rewriting, anti-unification, and counterexample-guided refinement. Evaluate whether generated lemmas can be certified and integrated into proof assistant workflows.

Develop a categorical semantics for a dependently typed language with effects, using comprehension categories or fibrations. Prove soundness of the syntax with respect to the model and examine how effect handlers interact with substitution and dependent elimination.

Investigate whether derived equivalences between finite-dimensional algebras preserve the asymptotic growth rate of Hochschild cohomology. Focus on constructing invariants detectable from the singularity category and compare with known behavior for self-injective algebras.

Study the structure of tensor ideals in the stable module category of a finite group scheme over a field of positive characteristic. Determine how support varieties classify thick tensor ideals beyond the finite group case.

Analyze the failure of unique factorization in Krull monoids arising from rings of algebraic integers with prescribed class group. Relate sets of lengths and elasticity to explicit arithmetic properties of the class group.

Develop criteria for when a triangulated category admitting a bounded t-structure is equivalent to the derived category of its heart. Examine obstruction classes coming from higher extensions and dg enhancements.

Investigate compactness properties of moduli spaces of flat connections on noncompact manifolds with cylindrical ends. Determine how boundary holonomy conditions affect the Uhlenbeck compactification.

Study the existence and uniqueness of minimal models for nilpotent topological spaces whose rational homotopy Lie algebras have prescribed growth. Relate polynomial versus exponential growth to formality and coformality phenomena.
Analyze the spectrum of the Laplace-Beltrami operator on degenerating families of hyperbolic surfaces. Derive precise asymptotics for small eigenvalues as collars pinch to nodes.

Explore the relationship between persistent homology and classical spectral sequences arising from filtered chain complexes. Determine when barcode decompositions recover extension data lost at the level of associated graded homology.
Investigate the distribution of rational points of bounded height on singular del Pezzo surfaces over number fields. Compare Peyre-type constants for different resolutions of singularities.
Study the behavior of Selmer groups in noncommutative Iwasawa extensions of elliptic curves with supersingular reduction. Formulate and test control theorems using signed Selmer conditions.

Analyze the zero distribution of families of automorphic L-functions near the central point under varying level. Determine consequences for low-lying zeros and symmetry type via explicit trace formula methods.

Investigate whether certain Galois representations attached to torsion classes in the cohomology of arithmetic groups satisfy expected local-global compatibility. Focus on ramifications at primes dividing the level.
Develop quantitative versions of the inverse Galois problem for solvable groups over Q. Seek bounds on discriminants of number fields realizing a fixed solvable group as Galois group.
Study equidistribution of Heegner points on Shimura curves in families with varying discriminant. Relate effective error terms to subconvexity bounds for Rankin-Selberg L-functions.
Analyze boundedness and compactness of multilinear singular integral operators on weighted Lebesgue spaces. Determine sharp dependence of operator norms on Muckenhoupt characteristics.

Investigate regularity thresholds for nonlinear dispersive equations with rough initial data on compact manifolds. Use probabilistic methods to compare almost sure well-posedness with deterministic critical scaling.
Study removable singularities for solutions of nonlinear elliptic systems with critical growth. Characterize singular sets using capacity estimates and monotonicity formulas.
Develop a microlocal analysis of wave propagation on manifolds with conic singularities. Determine how diffraction affects propagation of singularities and Strichartz estimates.
Analyze the boundary behavior of conformal maps between finitely connected planar domains with nonsmooth boundary. Relate prime end compactifications to Sobolev regularity of boundary traces.

Investigate ergodic properties of interval exchange transformations with prescribed combinatorics and restricted Rauzy-Veech induction. Determine quantitative mixing obstructions and deviations of Birkhoff sums.
Study classification of smooth actions of finite groups on exotic spheres in high dimensions. Compare equivariant surgery obstructions with fixed point data and stable homotopy invariants.
Analyze the relationship between K-theory of C*-algebras associated to groupoids and dynamical invariants of minimal Cantor systems. Determine when orbit equivalence is detected by ordered K-groups.

Investigate obstructions to the existence of symplectic fillings of contact 3-manifolds obtained by negative surgery on knots. Use Heegaard Floer correction terms and contact invariants to classify fillability.

Study higher Massey products in the cohomology of arrangement complements and their relation to resonance varieties. Determine whether nontrivial Massey products obstruct formality in specific reflection arrangements.
Analyze the structure of p-adic Banach representations of reductive groups beyond GL2(Qp). Seek explicit links between completed cohomology, locally analytic vectors, and eigenvariety geometry.
